texto: What Karen wants to do - needs to do - is cry, but she can't. Here, alone, when she could howl, beat the sofa cushions, scream; now, somehow, she is unable. It's for fear that if she gives in to it, she'll lose all sense of who she is. She is afraid that if she falls apart in private, then she'll fall apart completely. That if she crumbles, like a house in an earthquake, she will disappear down some deep, dark crevasse, and never be able to pull herself out and put herself back together again.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sarah Rayner: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “What Karen wants to do - needs to do - is cry, but she can't. Here, alone, when she could howl, b…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, anguish, grief pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: What Karen wants to do - needs to do - is cry, but she can't. Here, alone, when she could howl, beat the sofa cushions, scream; now, somehow, she is unable. It's for fear that if she gives in to it, she'll lose all sense of who she is. She is afraid that if she falls apart in private, then she'll fall apart completely. That if she crumbles, like a house in an earthquake, she will disappear down some deep, dark crevasse, and never be able to pull herself out and put herself back together again.

texto: She has no regrets; she knows now he could never have made her happy, even though he has, apparently, joined AA, is doing better. But sobriety is his journey, not hers; he needs to do it for himself, alone. Still, she misses him hugely, doesn't feel ready for another relationship yet. But as time passes, she hopes that she might be, eventually, with someone new, easier, kinder.
explicacao: Citação de Sarah Rayner sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “She has no regrets; she knows now he could never have made her happy, even though he has, apparen…” articula felicidade, love, moving-on em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como viagem e moving-on.

texto: Anna has a stab of jealousy - she is thrilled for them, yet can't help but envy their happiness. Don't be ungracious, she tells herself. It is not your time; it is theirs. Lou is so lovely; she deserves to be happy.
explicacao: Citação de Sarah Rayner sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Anna has a stab of jealousy - she is thrilled for them, yet can't help but envy their happiness. …” articula felicidade, happiness, jealousy em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como jealousy e love.

texto: She watches Simon's profile as he drives, concentrating, but he keeps turning to her, and every time he does so, he is smiling. He doesn't seem to care, and she wonders if, actually, he wants to be caught. In some ways she does, because she knows, already, albeit crazily swiftly, that she wants more of this man, that once was never, ever going to be enough.
